title: rhamnus pallasii subsp. iranica
intro: Rhamnus pallasii subsp. iranica, also known as the Iranian buckthorn or Persian buckthorn, is a subspecies of Rhamnus pallasii, a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East, including Iran. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anthraquinone glycosides and flavonoids, and is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rhamnus pallasii subsp. iranica include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its ability to support liver and kidney health. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, are often used to treat various health conditions, and it is also sometimes used as a natural remedy for skin and wound care.
